question_text: When reviewing classroom observations, which pattern concerns you the most?
option_a: Students finishing work at different times
option_b: Students asking many questions during the lesson
option_c: Students working quietly but showing little initiative

question_text: How do you usually understand students' varied learning preferences?
option_c: By noticing participation patterns in class visits

question_text: When walking into a classroom, what do you naturally look at first?
option_b: Whether the teacher is following the plan
option_c: Whether different students are engaged in different ways
option_d: Whether the class is quiet and orderly

question_text: If multiple classes struggle with a topic, what is your typical next step?
option_a: Check whether the chapters were completed
option_b: Compare the performance data across classes
option_c: Discuss with teachers how students approached the topic
option_d: Ask teachers to schedule additional revision periods

question_text: When looking at learning data, which trend do you investigate first?
option_a: Number of students who scored below 40%
option_c: Comparisons between sections of the same grade
option_d: Shifts in performance of specific students over time

question_text: During a class observation, if students complete a task very quickly, you generally think:
option_a: The task was easy and appropriate
option_b: The teacher's explanation was very clear
option_c: The task may not have challenged all learners
option_d: Students must have memorised similar questions

question_text: When reviewing lesson plans, which aspect do you notice most?
option_a: Whether the content matches the textbook
option_b: Whether the activities are manageable within the period
option_c: Whether the intended outcomes are measurable
option_d: Whether instructions are clear for the teacher to follow

question_text: A teacher asks for support in improving student understanding. Your first question is usually:
option_a: Which part of the syllabus is pending?
option_b: What tasks did students struggle with?
option_c: How did students respond during the lesson?
option_d: Which worksheets have been completed so far?

question_text: When teachers prepare activities, what is your main consideration?
option_a: Whether the activity is manageable for all classes
option_b: Whether the activity matches the exam format
option_c: Whether students must think in different ways during the activity
option_d: Whether the activity occupies the entire period

question_text: When you see a teacher using an unfamiliar method, what do you usually do?
option_a: Check whether the class control is maintained
option_c: Ask the teacher to explain the thinking behind it later
option_d: Remind them to stay aligned with the yearly plan

question_text: When a teacher struggles repeatedly with a concept, your instinctive response is:
option_a: Ask another teacher to share materials
option_c: Sit together to understand the root difficulty

question_text: During staff meetings, what do you tend to highlight the most?
When planning parent interactions, what is your priority?
option_c: Helping parents understand how learning happens
option_d: Addressing specific concerns raised by parents

question_text: When parents ask about their child's progress, you usually talk about:
option_c: How the child learns and participates

question_text: When designing the school communication plan, you focus on:
option_b: Ensuring messages are clear and short
option_c: Helping parents support learning at home
option_d: Making sure all notices reach parents
